channel.title: Blog on Christian Mahnke
channel.description: Recent content in Blog on Christian Mahnke

item.title: New biography of Franz Robert Fritz Neumann (1897-1984) aka Ric available
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description:
After the identity of Fritz Neumann and thus Ric could be clarified in October, Mark Strong from Meibohm Fine Arts compiled, condensed and published on AskArt on the basis of the findings from my blog.

Without a paid membership, the full biography can be viewed every Friday, otherwise it is also available for free at Meibohm Fine Arts.

Many thanks to Mark for his work!

item.title: "Landscape with pilasters and sculpture" ("Landschaft mit Pilaster und Skulptur") by Arnold Leissler the Younger (1939-2014)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I bought an etching by Arnold Leissler (the Younger) (1939-2014)…

item.title: Children by Elsa Haensgen-Dingkuhn (1898-1991)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I found a woodcut by Elsa Haensgen-Dingkuhn (1898-1991)…

item.title: DROHjanisches Pferd by Klaus Parche (1939-2024)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I have found an aquatint (etching) by Klaus Parche (1939-2024)…

item.title: Colour woodcut from Leo Frank (1884-1959)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I found a woodcut by Leo Frank (1884-1959)…

item.title: Quantum cosmos Göttingen
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Today (26 March) is the opening of the exhibition ‘Was zum Quant?!’ in the Forum Wissen. You can also see a digital exhibit in which I was involved…

item.title: Digital provenance for digitised cultural heritage
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: How can the authenticity of digitised cultural assets be guaranteed?

item.title: Never Built Göttingen
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Who doesn’t know them, the three blue towers of the University of Göttingen,…

item.title: AI and software "development"
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Over the past year, I have occasionally worked with AI to enrich my blogs with additional features and also to accelerate their further development.

item.title: Woodcut by Paul-Kurt Bartzsch (1917-1994)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I have found a woodcut by Paul-Kurt Bartzsch (1917-1994)…

item.title: Alte Mikrofone an neuer Hardware
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: In order to make podcasts in the future, I purchased some audio equipment in 2024…

item.title: Painting by Kaczmarek Tadeusz (1937-2006)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I have found a painting by Kaczmarek Tadeusz (automatic translation) (1937-2006)…

item.title: An unusual object
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I have once again found an interesting object…

item.title: IIIF Community Call 15.1.2025: HDR
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: After the Lightning Talk last year, now the next step…

item.title: Visualisation AI segmentation with HDR
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: The first step towards visually supported annotation with HDR…

item.title: Moscow drawings by Heinrich Sielken (1874 - 1942)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I came across two drawings by Heinrich Sielken (1874 - 1942)…

item.title: Summer evening in Worpswede (Sommerabend in Worpswede) by Cornelius Rogge (1868 – 1949)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I found an colourised etching by Cornelius Rogge (1868 – 1949)…

item.title: Projektemacher 2024
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: At the end of 2024, here is the annual retrospect for Projektemacher.org. Over 270 articles were published over the course of the year (the most active year to date).

item.title: Archives: Fostering collection development through citizen participation
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Archives are institutions of cultural memory - some more, some less…

item.title: Linoleum cut by Malte Satorius (1933-2017)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Random find: Christmas card from Malte Satorius (1933-2017).

item.title: Christmas 2024
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Merry Christmas to all readers, this year the traditional present comes a little earlier…

item.title: For the pre-Christmas period
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: To get in the mood for the pre-Christmas season…

item.title: Blog series on the topic of "light"
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Just right for the beginning of the dark season: One focus of the last year (2024) has been the topic of “light”. Starting with the historical 3D images in Vintage Reality to first attempts with Photogrammetry to HDR.

item.title: Craft workshop Christmas 2024
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: As my Christmas cards made from old wallpaper and paper have found a few fans in the meantime, I will be doing a workshop at Forum Wissen this year…

item.title: IIIF Online Meeting 2024: HDR Images via IIIF Image API
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: The slides of my presentation on HDR content via IIIF are now available…

item.title: Donation to the archive of the Spandau City History Museum
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: After the successful search for Fritz Neumann AKA Ric, it was time to make his work better known in Spandau. As a starting point, I donated a graphic to the archive of the Spandau City History Museum.

item.title: HDR images for the visualisation of X-ray examinations
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Another use case for HDR images…

item.title: Art Brut drawing from 1965
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I bought an interesting Art Brut drawing from 1965…

item.title: The mystery about Ric is solved
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: An old mystery finally solved…

item.title: Mastodon
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description:
Even if nothing has happened yet, I can now also be reached as @cmahnke@openbiblio.social via Mastodon.

And since nothing has happened on the Projektemacher Instagram channel for ages, I’ve replaced the link in the footer.

item.title: Haptic Feedback
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Another contribution from the Projektemacher Labs…

item.title: 911TV as desktop application
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I had some time, since I was ill:

item.title: “Seelischerkrankte Krankenhaus” (Mental health hospital)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I usually avoid buying reproductions…

item.title: IIIF Online Meeting 2024
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I will present my IIIF HDR project at this year’s IIIF Online Meeting!

item.title: Climate change in art...
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: …is “old news” by now: Neither when it comes to the effects that can be recognised in old works, nor as an object of art itself.

item.title: Ultraviolet Photogrammetry
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: This article presents the world’s first HDR model of UV photogrammetry in a browser.

item.title: HDR IIIF
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: At Zeitzeug the owner’s wallpaper collection is being sold and I have secured a particularly bright example…

item.title: "Liebespaar" (Lovers) by Max Westhäuser (1885-1956)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I have acquired a colour woodcut by Max Westhäuser (1885-1956)…

item.title: JavaScript library for HDR
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: The JavaScript implementation for the last posts is now also available as NPM module.

item.title: TTS for "Briefsteller"
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: After the Briefsteller blog had existed as a skeleton since the beginning of 2021, I used my summer holiday to bring the sample letter, which brought me to the topic “letter writing guides” in the first place, online…

item.title: Siedelhof Jena by Curt Mücke (1885-1940)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I found an colourised etching by Curt Mücke (1885-1940)…

item.title: Contrast enhancement for UV images using HDR
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: This example is about a practical use case for HDR visualisation in the browser…

item.title: LibUltraHDR
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: After research, it was necessary to explore some possible workflows and to create tools for a project in preparation. Based on Greg Benz’s preliminary work on HDR support, LibUltraHDR was the most sensible candidate for further experimentation.

item.title: Sheet by Imao Keinen (1855-1924)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I bought a Japanese colour woodcut by Imao Keinen (1855-1924)…

item.title: HDR images in the browser
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: For a project in preparation I have compiled a list of links to HDR on the web or in the browser.

item.title: Data Containers
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Even if it is not necessarily the intended use case, Docker makes it very easy to package large amounts of data with applications for simplified provisioning (such as APIs). This can be particularly helpful if, for example, standardisation data from different sources is to be merged for data enrichment.

item.title: Pong Visualisation
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: For some reason I came up with the idea of visualising Pong…

item.title: Etching by Kurt Mühlenhaupt (1921-2006)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I found an etching by Kurt Mühlenhaupt (1921-2006)…

item.title: VintageReality launched
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: The latest blog incubated at the Projektemacher Labs has been started.

item.title: 3D Models in the browser
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Maybe it will become a virtual exhibition about orange ceramics…

item.title: Etching by Marie Gey-Heinze (1881-1908)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I have acquired an etching by Marie Gey-Heinze (1881-1908)…

item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I bought a (more or less) mysterious box:

item.description: Merry Christmas to all readers, this year again there is a present…

item.description: I have acquired a linocut by Hans Martin Erhardt (1935-2015)…

item.description: For the video metadata of 911 TV there was an interesting challenge to solve…

item.description: I found a collage by Helmut Bönitz (1914-1999)…

item.title: 911TV started
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: An old idea of mine has now been realised:

item.title: Reisen wie 1996
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Sometimes you wonder how old the systems in trains are, here the passenger information system of an ICE 1 with an AMD 5x86 P-75 S CPU.

item.title: FarbholzschnittColour woodblock print by Oscar Droege (1898-1983)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I have found another colour woodcut by Oscar Droege (1898-1983)…

item.title: Windows Deployer
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description:
How to install a freshly built software from GitLab / GitHub?
On a Windows computer.
In an internal network - behind a VPN gateway

item.title: Woodcut by Erich Zimmer (1908-2001)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I have purchased a woodcut by Erich Zimmer (1908-2001)…

item.description: I have found a woodcut by Otto Richard Bossert (1874-1919)…

item.title: Build vector maps with GitHub
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Since the use of freely available map services requires communication with external services as well as limited design, it was time for something of my own…

item.title: GeoIP Tool
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Kibana visualisation with room plans needs data, e.g. from devices in a building. But the data has to come from somewhere…

item.title: OAI-PMH Harvester
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I didn’t want to write the millionth OAI-PMH harvester….

item.title: Colour woodcut from Eva Roemer (1889-1977)
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I found a woodcut by Eva Roemer (1889-1977)…

item.title: Hildesheimer Börde
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: The rest stop “Hildesheimer Börde” on a foggy night.

item.title: Maps for my Projektemacher blogs
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Another addition from Projektemacher Labs:

item.title: Donation to the city archive Halle
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Another donation to an archive should have arrived by now in city archives of Halle an der Saale. It concerns three postcards, the outlets of the Stadtgut Gimritz.

item.title: Christmas 2022
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Merry Christmas to all readers, this year there is another present…

item.title: Exhibition "Wilma Bräuner"
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: From December 6, the exhibition “Spurensuche im Sylter Archiv - Zum Leben und Werk von Wilma Bräuner (1891 - 1985)”, on which Evelyn Kraßmann and I worked together with a team, will take place in the city gallery “Alte Post”, Stephanstraße 4, Sylt / OT Westerland. As part of the preparations for the exhibition we have created a Wikipedia entry, the website is still under construction…

item.title: Hang pictures in Sweet Home 3D
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: For the planning of an exhibition using a 3D program, I have been looking into how pre-arranged picture frames could be imported into Sweet Home 3D…

item.title: Projektemacher Merch started
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Even though some of the designs are older, the time has finally come:

item.title: Computergrafik
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I had old computer graphics framed…

item.title: IIIF Proxy
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: To improve the ‘displayability’ of digital objects such as coins and books, I have written a proxy for IIIF resources…

item.title: Christmas 2021
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Merry Christmas to all readers, this year there is a present…

item.title: IIIF Conference 2021: Scaling down...
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Today I gave my talk about the infrastructure of my blogs at this year’s IIIF conference.

item.title: Instagram
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: As of today there is an additional Instagram channel. This is just an experiment for now. But what has already annoys me me: There doesn’t seem to be an official and stable API for creating posts / uploading images - very disappointing!

item.title: DIY Project: Phone case
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Today, once again, one of the rather rare non-digital projects: A cover for my new phone.

item.title: Summer break
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: This spring has not yet offered the most spring-like weather, but it should be better next week…

item.title: Briefsteller.de started
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Finally, the last of the blogs stared during the second Corona Lockdown is now online!

item.title: JPEG XL
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: Since my blogs are managed on GitHub, the images have to be stored as storage-saving as possible…

item.title: IIIF Konferenz 2021
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: I will present my Corona projects at this year’s IIIF conference!

item.title: Labs.projektemache.org gestartet
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: In order to have a page in the future that can easily break for a few days and to be able to present some things half-finished, there is now the Projektemacher Labs.

item.title: Living room renovated
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: In addition to my blogs, I used the corona period to renovate my living room. Here, too, I was able to realise some ideas that were years old:

item.title: Christmas 2020
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description: As the Advent season begins…

item.title: Der erste Beitrag
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description:
After the site Projektemacher.org went online in the last few months to unite my old Tumblr blogs and the projects of the first Corona Lockdown under a single

one roof as an umbrella brand, mine is now starting personal homepage.
